regions: Permian Basin (West Texas and Southeast New Mexico)
Anadarko, Ardmore, and Arkoma Basins (Oklahoma and South Central Kansas)
Louisiana Gulf Coast (onshore and near offshore)

dependencies: Third-party pipelines and facilities interconnected to natural gas and crude oil gathering systems
Third-party pipelines for delivery options to and from gathering and processing facilities
Third-party NGL pipelines, fractionators and storage facilities
risks: If third-party pipelines and other facilities become partially or fully unavailable, revenues could be adversely affected
Dependence on third-party facilities not within company control
Quality specifications changes at third-party facilities could restrict ability to utilize them

regulatory_risks: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) regulation under Natural Gas Act and Interstate Commerce Act
State regulations on natural gas gathering and crude oil operations (Texas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, New Mexico, North Dakota)
Tribal regulations on Fort Berthold Indian Reservation operations
Environmental laws: Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, CERCLA, RCRA, Oil Pollution Act, Safe Drinking Water Act, Endangered Species Act
Pipeline safety regulations under NGPSA and HLPSA by PHMSA
Climate change regulations including methane and GHG emissions standards
EPA regulations on air emissions, water discharges, waste disposal
State hydraulic fracturing regulations could restrict drilling activities
Occupational Safety and Health Act workplace standards
Potential changes in jurisdictional characterization of assets by regulatory agencies
FERC anti-market manipulation provisions with civil penalties up to $1.6 million per violation per day
State complaint-based regulation of natural gas transportation and gathering
Railroad Commission of Texas regulation of intrastate pipelines
Louisiana Public Service Commission regulation of common carrier pipelines
EPA Good Neighbor Plan rule affecting fossil fuel-fired power plants
Clean Water Act jurisdiction over waters of the United States and wetlands
Nationwide Permit 12 requirements under Clean Water Act
Inflation Reduction Act methane emissions fee ($900-$1,500 per ton)
Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT) at 15% on financial statement income
SEC climate risk disclosure requirements
California climate disclosure laws
Potential repeal or modification of environmental regulations under new Presidential administration
